get.description: Recent audit-trail events, newest first, each with the actor and, for an impersonated action, the real actor behind it. Read-only; gated by audit:read:admin (admin/owner only, since the audit trail is admin-sensitive).

parameters.description: Max rows to return, newest first (default 100, capped at 500)
parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Max rows to return, newest first (default 100, capped at 500)

parameters.description: Filter to one resource kind (e.g. auth, principal_grant)
parameters.name: resource

schema.description: Filter to one resource kind (e.g. auth, principal_grant)

parameters.description: Filter to one verb (e.g. login, create)
parameters.name: verb

schema.description: Filter to one verb (e.g. login, create)

parameters.description: Only events strictly older than this RFC3339 timestamp (paging backward)
parameters.name: before

schema.description: Only events strictly older than this RFC3339 timestamp (paging backward)

post.description: Verifies a human's password and sets an httpOnly session cookie. Public; a bad credential is a flat 401, and a correct password against a disabled account is a distinct 403 so the screen can explain it.
post.summary: Log in with a username and password

post.description: Revokes the session token and clears the cookie. Public.

get.description: Returns the caller's principal, flattened permissions (a UI hint and the fast-reject set), and grants. Requires authentication.
get.summary: The authenticated principal, its permissions, and grants

patch.description: Updates the caller's own display name (email is administrator-set). Requires authentication; self-scoped (edits only your own principal).

get.description: Returns the caller's profile picture as a base64-encoded JPEG. Requires authentication; self-scoped. No picture is a 404.

Lists the caller's own active bearer credentials (time-bounded web-login sessions and CLI/API tokens) with their non-secret metadata; the current one is flagged. Requires authentication; self-scoped (never another principal's). The token secret is never returned.
get.summary: List your own sessions and tokens

post.description: Revokes one of the caller's own sessions or tokens by id (from the session list); revoking the current one signs it out. Requires authentication; self-scoped, so a credential id that is not yours is a 404.

parameters.description: The credential id to revoke (from the session list)
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The credential id to revoke (from the session list)

post.summary: Revoke one of your own sessions

post.description: Revokes every one of the caller's own web-login sessions, or every one of its CLI/API tokens (chosen by purpose), returning how many were ended. Requires authentication; self-scoped. Always keeps the credential that made this request, so you are never signed out of the one you are on; sessions and tokens never cross.
post.summary: Revoke all of your own sessions or tokens

post.description: Mints a CLI/API token for the caller and returns it once (store it now; it cannot be retrieved again). A description is required (what the token is for); an optional ttl_days bounds its lifetime (default 90, maximum 365). Requires authentication; self-scoped (always issued for you). The token is stamped with the device and address that created it.
post.summary: Create one of your own API tokens

post.description: Verifies the current password and sets a new one. Requires authentication; self-scoped.
Clears the caller's profile picture. Requires authentication; self-scoped.
Sets the caller's profile picture (JPEG, PNG, or WebP, base64-encoded), normalized server-side to a 256x256 JPEG. Requires authentication; self-scoped. A bad or oversize image is a 422.
Revokes the impersonation session presented by the request token, ending the view-as / act-as. Requires an impersonation token.

get.description: Public: reports whether any owner has been bootstrapped, so the login screen can hide the bootstrap hint.
get.summary: Whether the system has an owner yet

get.description: Lists the capability registry, ordered alphabetically by display name. Populates the capability picker on the product form. Gated by capability:read.

post.description: Creates a custom (non-official) capability. Gated by capability:create.

delete.description: Deletes a custom capability, refused if official (422). Gated by capability:delete.

get.description: Fetches a capability by id. Gated by capability:read.

patch.description: Patches a custom capability's display_name. Official capabilities are read-only (422). Gated by capability:update.

get.description: Lists the components the caller may read, each filtered to its scope subtree. Gated by component:read.

post.description: Creates a component, optionally under a parent (a root needs an all-scoped grant), bound to a system and a location. Gated by component:create.

delete.description: Deletes a component, refused while it still has child components. Gated by component:delete; read and delete scopes drive the 404 versus 403 split.

get.description: Fetches a component by name within the caller's read scope. Out of scope is a non-disclosing 404. Gated by component:read.

patch.description: Patches a component's technical name or display_name. Gated by component:update; read and update scopes drive the 404 versus 403 split.

get.description: What this component actually provides: the capabilities its product declares, plus the ones the component adds, minus the ones it suppresses. This is the set the role-assignment guard checks, so a productless component that declares its own can still be staffed. Gated by component:read; an out-of-scope component is a non-disclosing 404.

delete.description: Removes the component's own fact about the capability, so it falls back to whatever its product declares. Clearing a fact the component never declared is a 404. Gated by component:update; an out-of-scope component is a non-disclosing 404.
delete.summary: Clear a capability declaration on a component

put.description: Records this component's own fact about a capability: present true adds one its product does not claim, present false suppresses one it does. Idempotent. An unknown capability is a 422; an unknown or out-of-scope component is a non-disclosing 404 (the component is resolved in scope first). Gated by component:update.
put.summary: Declare a capability on a component

get.description: Resolves the tags that cascade onto a component (global -> location -> system -> component): keys union, values override most-specific-wins, with the winner and shadowed candidates. A non-propagating key resolves only from a binding on the component itself. Gated by component:read; the component must be in the caller's component read scope.
Returns the component's recent log occurrences (the log-kind sink), newest first, bounded to the last 24 hours. Gated by component:read; an out-of-scope component is a non-disclosing 404.

Every property the component's product declares, resolved to the component's own value or the contract default (is_set marks the override), plus any property set directly on the component (from_contract false). Gated by component:read; an out-of-scope component is a non-disclosing 404.

delete.description: Removes the component's declared value, so the property falls back to the product contract's default (or leaves the effective read entirely when it was off-contract). Clearing a property the component never set is a 404. Gated by component:update; an out-of-scope component is a non-disclosing 404.
delete.summary: Clear a property on a component

put.description: Declares a value for the property on this component, overriding the product contract's default. Idempotent: the first set stores the value, a later set replaces it. The property need not be on the contract, but it must exist in the catalog (422 otherwise). Gated by component:update; an out-of-scope component is a non-disclosing 404.
put.summary: Set a property on a component

get.description: Composes, per interface, the latest reachability verdict, the probe-layer signals that compose it, and the recent verdict transitions for the availability strip. Gated by component:read; an out-of-scope component is a non-disclosing 404.
Lists the tags bound directly on a component (not the resolved cascade). Gated by component:read.

post.description: Removes a key's value from a component. Gated by component:update.
post.summary: Remove a tag value from a component

post.description: Binds a value for a key on a component. The key must exist and apply to this entity kind. Setting a value is the ordinary entity write, gated by component:update.
post.summary: Set a tag value on a component

post.description: Reports whether a proposed technical name is a valid slug and currently free. Advisory (Save is still gated by the unique constraint). Availability is scope-blind to match the global unique constraint. Gated by component:update.

get.description: Lists the driver registry, ordered alphabetically by display name. Populates the driver picker on the product form. Gated by driver:read.

post.description: Creates a custom (non-official) driver. Gated by driver:create.

delete.description: Deletes a custom driver, refused if official (422). Gated by driver:delete.

get.description: Fetches a driver by id. Gated by driver:read.

patch.description: Patches a custom driver's display_name or version. Official drivers are read-only (422). Gated by driver:update.

get.description: Lists the file handles the caller may see (searchable metadata, no bytes). Sensitive files appear only to the admin tier. Gated by file:read.

post.description: Stores the uploaded bytes as a content-addressed blob (identical bytes dedup to one blob) and writes the file handle pointing at it. Gated by file:create; a sensitive file additionally needs the admin tier (file:create:admin).
post.summary: Create a file from an upload

delete.description: Removes a file handle. The underlying blob is left in place (garbage collection is a later slice). A sensitive file is a non-disclosing 404 without the admin tier. Gated by file:delete.

get.description: Returns one file handle's searchable metadata (no bytes). A sensitive file is a non-disclosing 404 without the admin tier. Gated by file:read.
Returns a file's bytes (base64-encoded) read from the blob it points at, the hash verified on read. A sensitive file is a non-disclosing 404 without the admin tier. Gated by file:read.
Reports process health and the database leg, pinged through the Storage Gateway.
Lists the interfaces whose owning component the caller may read (the component cascade). Gated by interface:read.

post.description: Creates an interface owned by a component (or a server-hosted one, which needs an all-scoped grant). The create scope cascades through the owning component. Gated by interface:create.

delete.description: Deletes an interface, refused while a task still references it. Gated by interface:delete; read and delete scopes (through the component) drive the 404 versus 403 split.

get.description: Fetches an interface by id. An interface whose component is out of the caller's read scope is a non-disclosing 404. Gated by interface:read.

patch.description: Patches an interface's node placement or params. Gated by interface:update; read and update scopes (through the component) drive the 404 versus 403 split.

get.description: Lists the location type's declared-property contract (what every location of the type exposes), ordered by property name, each with its optional default and required flag. Gated by type:read.
get.summary: List a location type's declared properties

delete.description: Removes one line from a custom location type's contract; locations of the type keep any value they set for it, now off-contract. A property the type does not declare is a 404, and an official type is read-only (422). Gated by type:delete.
delete.summary: Withdraw a property from a location type

put.description: Declares a catalog property on a custom location type, or revises the declaration in place (the line is addressed by name, so the write is idempotent). Official location types are read-only (422); an unknown type is a 404 and a property the catalog does not know is a 422. Gated by type:update.
put.summary: Declare a property on a location type

get.description: Lists the locations the caller may read, each filtered to its scope subtree. Gated by location:read.

post.description: Creates a location, optionally under a parent (a root needs an all-scoped grant). Gated by location:create.

delete.description: Deletes a location, refused while it still has child locations. Gated by location:delete; read and delete scopes drive the 404 versus 403 split.

get.description: Fetches a location by name within the caller's read scope. Out of scope is a non-disclosing 404. Gated by location:read.

patch.description: Patches a location's display_name, location_type, or parent (a move). Gated by location:update; the read and update scopes drive the 404 versus 403 split.

get.description: Every property the location's type declares, resolved to the location's own value or the contract default (is_set marks the override), plus any property set directly on the location (from_contract false). Gated by location:read; an out-of-scope location is a non-disclosing 404.

delete.description: Removes the location's declared value, so the property falls back to the location type contract's default (or leaves the effective read entirely when it was off-contract). Clearing a property the location never set is a 404. Gated by location:update; an out-of-scope location is a non-disclosing 404.
delete.summary: Clear a property on a location

put.description: Declares a value for the property on this location, overriding the location type contract's default. Idempotent: the first set stores the value, a later set replaces it. The property need not be on the contract, but it must exist in the catalog (422 otherwise). Gated by location:update; an out-of-scope location is a non-disclosing 404.
put.summary: Set a property on a location

get.description: Lists the tags bound directly on a location (not the resolved cascade). Gated by location:read.

post.description: Removes a key's value from a location. Gated by location:update.
post.summary: Remove a tag value from a location

post.description: Binds a value for a key on a location. The key must exist and apply to this entity kind. Setting a value is the ordinary entity write, gated by location:update.
post.summary: Set a tag value on a location

post.description: Reports whether a proposed technical name is a valid slug and currently free. Advisory (Save is still gated by the unique constraint). Availability is scope-blind to match the global unique constraint. Gated by location:update.

get.description: Lists the edge nodes. A node is estate-wide, so listing requires an all-scope read. Gated by node:read.

post.description: Registers an edge node server-side (day-one enrollment: create, then :enroll to mint its token). Gated by node:create.

delete.description: Decommissions a node: a hard delete that cascades its interfaces, their derived tasks, its node-owned tags and self-telemetry, and its enrollment credential. Component telemetry it collected is unaffected. Requires an all-scope action. Gated by node:delete.

get.description: Fetches a node by name. Requires an all-scope read. Gated by node:read.

patch.description: Patches a node's display name, description, and location (a nil field is unchanged; a location of "" clears it). The name is immutable. Requires an all-scope action. Gated by node:update.

post.description: Mints (or re-mints) the node's enrollment token and returns it once. The token is stored only as a hash; it is never logged. Gated by node:enroll.

get.description: Lists the tags bound directly on a node (not the resolved cascade). Gated by node:read.

post.description: Removes a key's value from a node. Gated by node:update.
post.summary: Remove a tag value from a node

post.description: Binds a value for a key on a node. The key must exist and apply to this entity kind. Setting a value is the ordinary entity write, gated by node:update.
post.summary: Set a tag value on a node

post.description: The node-facing exchange: a node presents its enrollment token and receives its NATS credential (url, username, password). Public (the token is the authentication); an invalid token is a 401.
post.summary: Claim a node identity in exchange for its NATS credential

get.description: Every principal group. Gated by principal_group:read:admin.

post.description: Creates a principal group. Gated by principal_group:create (all-scope). A duplicate name is 409.

delete.description: Removes a group and, by cascade, its memberships and grants. Gated by principal_group:delete (all-scope).

get.description: One principal group by id. Gated by principal_group:read:admin.

patch.description: Updates a group's name and presentational fields. Gated by principal_group:update (all-scope). A duplicate name is 409.

get.description: The role x scope grants a group confers on its members. Gated by principal_group:read:admin.

post.description: Assigns a role at a scope to a group; its members inherit it. Gated by principal_grant:create (all-scope). Refused (403) when the granted role's capabilities exceed the granter's own, exactly as for a direct grant. A duplicate is 409.
post.summary: Grant a role to a group

delete.description: Removes one grant from a group. Gated by principal_grant:delete (all-scope).

get.description: The principals in a group. Gated by principal_group:read:admin.

post.description: Adds a principal to a group; its members inherit the group's grants. Gated by principal_group:update (all-scope). Idempotent.
post.summary: Add a member to a group

delete.description: Removes a principal from a group; it stops inheriting the group's grants. Gated by principal_group:update (all-scope).
delete.summary: Remove a member from a group

get.description: Lists all principals (humans and service accounts) with their grants. Gated by principal:read:admin.

parameters.description: Include archived (soft-deleted) principals, hidden by default
parameters.name: include_archived

schema.description: Include archived (soft-deleted) principals, hidden by default

post.description: Creates a human principal with an optional initial password. Gated by principal:create (all-scope). The new principal holds no grants; assign roles separately.

get.description: Fetches one principal by id with its profile and grants. Gated by principal:read:admin.

parameters.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username

patch.description: Updates a human principal's display name, email, and username. Gated by principal:update (all-scope). Renaming is safe: nothing keys on the username.

parameters.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username

get.description: Returns the principal's profile picture as a base64-encoded JPEG. Gated by principal:read:admin. A principal without a picture is a 404.

parameters.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username

post.description: Assigns a role at a scope to a principal. Gated by principal_grant:create (all-scope). Refused (403) when the granted role's capabilities exceed the granter's own (no promoting anyone, including yourself, to a higher tier such as owner). A duplicate is 409, an unknown role or bad scope 422.

parameters.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username

post.summary: Grant a role to a principal

delete.description: Removes one grant from a principal. Gated by principal_grant:delete (all-scope). The last owner grant cannot be revoked.

parameters.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username

get.description: Lists another principal's active bearer credentials (login sessions and API tokens) with their non-secret metadata, newest first, so an administrator can see where an account is signed in and revoke a session that should not be. Gated by principal:revoke-session (all-scope). The token secret is never returned, and current is always false (there is no "this request's own session" when viewing another principal).

parameters.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username

post.description: Revokes one of another principal's sessions or tokens by id (an administrator action; the target is immediately signed out of that credential). Gated by principal:revoke-session (all-scope). Bounded to the target, so a credential id that is not theirs is a non-disclosing 404, never a cross-principal revoke. Refused (403) on an owner (an owner's sessions cannot be revoked by anyone, the takeover guard shared with impersonation and password reset) or when it would exceed the caller's own capabilities. Audited with the administrator as the actor.

parameters.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username

parameters.description: The credential id to revoke (from the principal's session list)
parameters.name: sid

schema.description: The credential id to revoke (from the principal's session list)

post.description: Revokes every one of another principal's web-login sessions, or every one of its CLI/API tokens (chosen by purpose), in a single administrator action, returning how many were ended. Gated by principal:revoke-session (all-scope). Bounded to the target and never crosses purpose (revoking sessions leaves tokens, and vice versa). Refused (403) on an owner (the takeover guard shared with impersonation and the password reset) or when it would exceed the caller's own capabilities. Audited with the administrator as the actor.

parameters.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username

post.summary: Revoke all of a principal's sessions or tokens

post.description: Soft-deletes a principal: it is hidden from the directory, can no longer authenticate, and its rows stay intact, reversibly (restore) until purged. Gated by principal:archive (all-scope). The last active owner cannot be archived.

parameters.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username

post.description: Soft-disables a principal so it can no longer authenticate; its audit trail is kept. Gated by principal:update (all-scope). The last active owner cannot be disabled.

parameters.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username

post.description: Re-enables a disabled principal, restoring its ability to authenticate. Gated by principal:update (all-scope).

parameters.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username

post.description: Mints a bounded, revocable token to view as (read-only) or act as (full) the target. Gated by principal:impersonate (all-scope). Refused on self, on an owner target (owners are un-impersonatable by anyone), when it would grant a capability the caller lacks (the escalation guard), or from within an existing impersonation.

parameters.description: The principal to impersonate, addressed by its uuid or a human username
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The principal to impersonate, addressed by its uuid or a human username

post.description: Hard-deletes an archived principal and its owned rows (profile, credentials, grants, memberships); the audit trail is preserved. Irreversible. Gated by principal:purge (admin-sensitive, all-scope), and the principal must be archived first.

parameters.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username

post.description: Clears another human principal's profile picture. Gated by principal:set-avatar (all-scope). Removing an absent picture is a no-op. Audited with the administrator as the actor.

parameters.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username

post.description: Sets a new password for another human principal (an administrator action; the target's current password is not required). Gated by principal:reset-password (all-scope). The new password must meet the password policy; a violation is a 422. Refused on yourself (change your own password from your profile, which verifies your current one), on an owner (owners cannot be reset by anyone), or when it would exceed the caller's own capabilities (the takeover guard, shared with impersonation). The action is audited with the administrator as the actor.

parameters.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username

post.description: Reverses an archive: the account is restored to active and can authenticate again. Gated by principal:archive (all-scope).

parameters.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username

post.description: Sets another human principal's profile picture (an administrator action). Gated by principal:set-avatar (all-scope). The image (JPEG, PNG, or WebP, base64-encoded) is normalized server-side to a 256x256 JPEG; a bad or oversize image is a 422. Audited with the administrator as the actor.

parameters.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username
parameters.name: id

schema.description: The principal, addressed by its uuid or a human username

get.description: Lists the product registry, ordered alphabetically by display name. Each product carries its vendor, driver, kind, and capabilities. Gated by product:read.

post.description: Creates a custom (non-official) product and sets its capabilities. Gated by product:create.

delete.description: Deletes a custom product, refused if official (422) or still referenced by a component (409). Gated by product:delete.

get.description: Fetches a product by id, with its capabilities. Gated by product:read.

patch.description: Patches a custom product's display_name, vendor, driver, kind, or parent, and replaces its capabilities when provided. Official products are read-only (422). Gated by product:update.

get.description: Lists the product's declared-property contract (what every instance of the product exposes), ordered by property name, each with its optional default and required flag. Gated by product:read.

delete.description: Removes one line from a custom product's contract; instances keep any value they set for it, now off-contract. A property the product does not declare is a 404, and an official product is read-only (422). Gated by product:delete.
delete.summary: Withdraw a property from a product

put.description: Declares a catalog property on a custom product, or revises the declaration in place (the line is addressed by name, so the write is idempotent). Official products are read-only (422), and an unknown product or property is a 422. Gated by product:update.
put.summary: Declare a property on a product

get.description: Lists every registered property (official and custom). The catalog is estate-wide reference data. Gated by property:read.

post.description: Registers a custom property (official=false). The name must be a valid property key. Gated by property:create.

delete.description: Removes a custom property by name. Official properties are read-only. Gated by property:delete.

get.description: Returns one property by name. Gated by property:read.

patch.description: Patches a custom property's label, description, unit, or validation (a nil field is unchanged). Data type and kind are fixed at creation. Official properties are read-only. Gated by property:update.

get.description: Lists the roles with their metadata and effective (flattened) permissions. Gated by the role:read:admin capability.

Lists the secrets the caller may see, with masked fields, filtered to the read scope; admin-sensitive secrets appear only to the admin tier. Gated by secret:read, which the viewer floor does not carry (secret is a sensitive resource).

post.description: Seals a secret at an owner scope (a global secret needs an all-scoped grant). Fields are validated and encrypted against the type shape. Gated by secret:create.

delete.description: Removes a secret by id. Gated by secret:delete; read and delete scopes on the owner drive the 404 versus 403 split.

patch.description: Replaces the given field values on a secret, re-sealing secret fields. Only values change; name, type, and owner are fixed at creation. An omitted field keeps its value. Gated by secret:update.

post.description: Decrypts and returns a secret's field values for a clipboard copy, audited under the copy verb (distinct from an on-screen reveal). Same exposure and the same secret:reveal gate as reveal.
post.summary: Decrypt a secret for clipboard copy

post.description: Decrypts and returns a secret's field values, auditing the decrypt. Gated by secret:reveal at the caller's scope; an admin-sensitive secret additionally needs the admin tier (secret:reveal:admin), so a scoped operator reveals device secrets but never a platform credential.

get.description: The effective settings document plus per-key provenance (which level won) and lock state. Gated by settings:read (admin).
The current principal's resolved settings, client-visible namespaces only, no provenance. Feeds the SPA at boot. Requires authentication.

delete.description: Drops the namespace's global override, restoring file and code defaults. Gated by settings:update.
delete.summary: Restore a settings namespace to defaults

patch.description: Applies an RFC 7386 JSON Merge Patch to the namespace's global override; null on a key restores it. Gated by settings:update.

schema.description: an RFC 7386 JSON Merge Patch for the namespace; null on a key restores it

post.description: Removes every global override (a factory reset). Gated by settings:update.

get.description: Lists the standard catalog, ordered alphabetically by display name. A standard is the blueprint a system conforms to. Gated by standard:read.

post.description: Creates a custom (non-official) standard, optionally as a variant of another. Gated by standard:create.

delete.description: Deletes a custom standard, refused if official (422) or still referenced by a system (409). Gated by standard:delete.

get.description: Fetches a standard by id. Gated by standard:read.

patch.description: Patches a custom standard's display_name or parent. Official standards are read-only (422). Gated by standard:update.

get.description: Lists the standard's declared-property contract (what every system conforming to it exposes), ordered by property name, each with its optional default and required flag. Gated by standard:read.

delete.description: Removes one line from a custom standard's contract; conforming systems keep any value they set for it, now off-contract. A property the standard does not declare is a 404, and an official standard is read-only (422). Gated by standard:delete.
delete.summary: Withdraw a property from a standard

put.description: Declares a catalog property on a custom standard, or revises the declaration in place (the line is addressed by name, so the write is idempotent). Official standards are read-only (422); an unknown standard is a 404 and a property the catalog does not know is a 422. Gated by standard:update.
put.summary: Declare a property on a standard

get.description: Lists the roles this standard declares (every conforming system inherits them live), ordered by name, each with its quorum and the capabilities a component must provide to fill it. Gated by standard:read.

delete.description: Removes the role from the standard, and with it every assignment conforming systems made to it. A role the standard does not declare is a 404. Gated by standard:delete.
delete.summary: Withdraw a role from a standard

put.description: Declares a role every conforming system needs filled, or revises it in place (the role is addressed by name, so the write is idempotent). The capability list replaces the required set wholesale. An unknown standard or capability is a 422. Gated by standard:update.
put.summary: Declare a role on a standard

get.description: Lists the systems the caller may read, each filtered to its scope subtree. Gated by system:read.

post.description: Creates a system, optionally under a parent (a root needs an all-scoped grant) and at a location. Gated by system:create.

delete.description: Deletes a system, refused while it still has child systems. Gated by system:delete; read and delete scopes drive the 404 versus 403 split.

get.description: Fetches a system by name within the caller's read scope. Out of scope is a non-disclosing 404. Gated by system:read.

patch.description: Patches a system's display_name or standard. An omitted standard_id leaves it unchanged; an explicit empty string clears it, converting the system to a one-off. Gated by system:update; read and update scopes drive the 404 versus 403 split.

get.description: Every property the system's standard declares, resolved to the system's own value or the contract default (is_set marks the override), plus any property set directly on the system (from_contract false). Gated by system:read; an out-of-scope system is a non-disclosing 404.

delete.description: Removes the system's declared value, so the property falls back to the standard contract's default (or leaves the effective read entirely when it was off-contract). Clearing a property the system never set is a 404. Gated by system:update; an out-of-scope system is a non-disclosing 404.
delete.summary: Clear a property on a system

put.description: Declares a value for the property on this system, overriding the standard contract's default. Idempotent: the first set stores the value, a later set replaces it. The property need not be on the contract, but it must exist in the catalog (422 otherwise). Gated by system:update; an out-of-scope system is a non-disclosing 404.
put.summary: Set a property on a system

get.description: Every role this system needs filled: those its standard declares (from_standard true) plus those declared directly on it, each with the capabilities it requires, the components filling it, and how many more it wants before quorum (understaffed). A one-off system shows only its own. Gated by system:read; an out-of-scope system is a non-disclosing 404.

delete.description: Removes a role declared on this system, and with it every assignment to it. A role the system does not declare itself is a 404 (a role inherited from its standard is withdrawn on the standard, not here). Gated by system:update; an out-of-scope system is a non-disclosing 404.
delete.summary: Withdraw a role from a system

put.description: Declares a role directly on this system (how a one-off system gets roles at all, and how a conforming one adds what its standard does not cover), or revises it in place. The capability list replaces the required set wholesale. Gated by system:update; an out-of-scope system is a non-disclosing 404.
put.summary: Declare a role on a system

delete.description: Takes this component out of the role, leaving the role understaffed until another fills it. A component that was not filling the role is a 404. Gated by system:update; an out-of-scope system is a non-disclosing 404.
delete.summary: Unassign a component from a role

put.description: Puts this component in the role for this system. Refused with a 422 naming the missing capabilities when the component does not provide everything the role requires (its product's capabilities, plus what it adds, minus what it suppresses). Idempotent. Gated by system:update; an out-of-scope system is a non-disclosing 404.
put.summary: Assign a component to a role

get.description: Lists the tags bound directly on a system (not the resolved cascade). Gated by system:read.

post.description: Removes a key's value from a system. Gated by system:update.
post.summary: Remove a tag value from a system

post.description: Binds a value for a key on a system. The key must exist and apply to this entity kind. Setting a value is the ordinary entity write, gated by system:update.
post.summary: Set a tag value on a system

post.description: Reports whether a proposed technical name is a valid slug and currently free. Advisory (Save is still gated by the unique constraint). Availability is scope-blind to match the global unique constraint. Gated by system:update.

get.description: Lists the governed key vocabulary. Rides the tag:read floor.

post.description: Adds a key to the governed vocabulary. The name is normalized (a lowercase identifier). Gated by tag:create (all-scope, an admin action).

delete.description: Removes a key from the vocabulary, cascading its bindings. Gated by tag:delete (all-scope).

patch.description: Replaces a key's governance fields (applies_to, propagates); the name is fixed. Gated by tag:update (all-scope).

post.description: Removes the global binding for a key. Gated by tag:update (all-scope).
Binds a tenant-wide default value for a key at the global scope. Gated by tag:update (all-scope).

get.description: Returns the distinct values already bound for a key across the estate, for value autocomplete on a free-text key (an enum key carries its allowed set on the key itself). Rides the tag:read floor.
get.summary: List the distinct values bound for a key

get.description: Lists the tasks whose interface's owning component the caller may read (the component cascade). Tasks are derived from interfaces, not authored. Gated by task:read.
Fetches a task by id. A task whose component is out of the caller's read scope is a non-disclosing 404. Gated by task:read.
Lists the location_type registry (the shape-definers a location is classified by), ordered alphabetically by display name. Populates the type picker on the location form. Gated by type:read.

post.description: Creates a custom (non-official) location_type. Gated by type:create.

delete.description: Deletes a custom location_type, refused if official (422) or still referenced by a location (409). Gated by type:delete.

patch.description: Patches a custom location_type's display_name or icon. Official types are read-only (422). Gated by type:update.

get.description: Lists the secret_type shapes a secret can take, for the create form. Gated by secret:read.
Lists every variable. Requires an all-scope read. Gated by variable:read.

post.description: Sets a variable at an owner scope (a global variable needs an all-scoped grant). The value is validated against value_type. Gated by variable:create.

delete.description: Removes a variable by id. Gated by variable:delete; read and delete scopes on the owner drive the 404 versus 403 split.

patch.description: Replaces a variable's value, validated against its fixed value_type. Only the value changes; name, type, and owner are fixed at creation. Gated by variable:update.

get.description: Lists the vendor registry, ordered alphabetically by display name. Populates the vendor picker on the product form. Gated by vendor:read.

post.description: Creates a custom (non-official) vendor. Gated by vendor:create.

delete.description: Deletes a custom vendor, refused if official (422). Gated by vendor:delete.

get.description: Fetches a vendor by id. Gated by vendor:read.

patch.description: Patches a custom vendor's display_name, kind, icon, support_phone, or website. Official vendors are read-only (422). Gated by vendor:update.
